features.summary: Broadcom reported consolidated net revenue of $8.9 billion for Q3 2023, a 5% increase year-on-year. Semiconductor solutions revenue was $6.9 billion, also up 5%, while infrastructure software revenue grew to $1.9 billion, reflecting a 5% year-on-year increase. The company anticipates Q4 revenue of $9.27 billion, up 4% year-on-year, driven by strong demand in generative AI and networking technologies.
features.management_outlook: Management expects networking revenue to accelerate over 20% year-on-year in Q4, primarily due to generative AI deployments. They forecast semiconductor revenue growth of low-to-mid single digits year-on-year, with a stable run rate of approximately $6 billion excluding AI. The company remains optimistic about the upcoming acquisition of VMware, expecting to close the deal on October 30, 2023.
features.risks: Broadcom highlighted potential risks related to macroeconomic conditions affecting enterprise and telco spending, which have moderated. Additionally, the company noted supply chain constraints for generative AI products due to long lead times and the complexity of production, which could impact future growth.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of growth include the increasing demand for generative AI technologies, which has significantly boosted networking revenue. The company's investments in advanced silicon technologies and custom AI compute engines for hyperscale customers are also contributing to revenue growth. Furthermore, strong renewal rates in infrastructure software and a diversified product portfolio across various end markets support overall performance.
